name: Zerodha Trader Hub — Advanced Redis Data Structures
scenario: Build the Zerodha trader hub with three Redis patterns in production: a real-time P&L leaderboard (Sorted Set), instrument metadata cache with null-object protection (cache-aside), and user session storage (Hash). Include stampede-safe market status loading.
description: Redis Sorted Set leaderboard + manual cache-aside with null sentinel + Redis Hash sessions + stampede prevention. Integration tests verify rank order, null sentinel prevents DB hits, and session fields are accessible individually.

core.description: TraderLeaderboard @Service: Redis Sorted Set 'leaderboard:pnl'. recordTrade(traderId, pnl): ZINCRBY. getTopN(n): ZREVRANGE. getMyRank(traderId): ZREVRANK → 1-indexed position. resetBoard(): DEL. getTotalParticipants(): ZCARD.

InstrumentCacheService @Service: manual cache-aside 'instrument:{symbol}'. findBySymbol(): null sentinel '__NONE__' (5-min TTL) for unknown symbols. Real instruments cached for 6 hours. onNewInstrument(): evict sentinel, cache new instrument.

UserSessionCache @Service: Redis Hash 'session:{userId}'. saveSession(userId, role, email, merchantId): HSET all fields + 30-min expiry. getRole(userId): HGET. extendSession(userId): EXPIRE reset. invalidate(userId): DEL.
MarketStatusService @Service: stampede-safe getStatus(). Key 'market:status', TTL=30s, mutex lock TTL=5s. Double-check after acquiring lock. Returns 'OPEN'/'CLOSED'/'PRE_OPEN'.

Integration tests (4): (1) recordTrade × 3, verify getTopN returns correct order, (2) findBySymbol('INVALID') twice → DB called once (null sentinel prevents second), (3) saveSession then getRole → correct role returned, (4) getTopN after resetBoard → empty list. All 4 pass.

bonus.description: DailyLeaderboardReset @Scheduled: runs at 15:30 IST (market close). Snapshots top 10 to LeaderboardHistory @Entity (PostgreSQL). Then DEL the Redis key. @DataJpaTest verifying snapshot persistence.

Add Redis List activity feed: TraderActivityFeed @Service. pushActivity(userId, description): LPUSH + LTRIM to 50. getRecentActivities(userId, n): LRANGE. Integration test: 60 activities pushed, only 50 retained.

Multi-level cache: add Caffeine L1 in front of InstrumentCacheService. L1=50 entries, 60s TTL. On L1 miss: check Redis (L2). On L2 miss: DB. On update: invalidate both L1 and L2. Test verifying hit hierarchy.

data_to_use:
Leaderboard test:
  Aisha Sharma: 3 trades → ₹5,000 + ₹2,000 - ₹500 = ₹6,500 P&L
  Raj Patel:    2 trades → ₹3,000 + ₹1,500 = ₹4,500 P&L
  Priya Verma:  1 trade  → ₹2,000 P&L
  Expected top 3: [Aisha, Raj, Priya]

Null sentinel test:
  findBySymbol('XYZINVALID') → not in DB → store __NONE__
  findBySymbol('XYZINVALID') again → return empty (no DB call)

Session test:
  saveSession('U001', 'TRADER', 'aisha@zerodha.com', null)
  getRole('U001') → 'TRADER'
example_output:
# Leaderboard test:
recordTrade('Aisha', 5000) → ZINCRBY leaderboard:pnl 5000 Aisha
recordTrade('Aisha', 2000) → ZINCRBY leaderboard:pnl 2000 Aisha
recordTrade('Raj',   3000) → ZINCRBY leaderboard:pnl 3000 Raj
getTopN(3) → ['Aisha', 'Raj']

# Null sentinel test:
findBySymbol('XYZINVALID') → DB QUERY (miss) → cache __NONE__
findBySymbol('XYZINVALID') → cache hit: __NONE__ → no DB query

# Session test:
saveSession('U001', 'TRADER', ...) → HSET session:U001 role TRADER ...
getRole('U001') → HGET session:U001 role → 'TRADER'

# Tests:
✓ leaderboard_topNInCorrectOrder
✓ nullSentinel_preventsSecondDbQuery
✓ session_roleReturnedCorrectly
✓ resetBoard_returnsEmptyTopN
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. reverseRank is 0-indexed — add 1 for human-readable position 1. null sentinel check: NONE.equals(cached) must come before instanceof check (both could match Object). Sorted Set uses String template; Hash+List can use String template; Object template for complex objects (instruments). Lock TTL must be long enough for DB query to complete (5-10 seconds). Try-finally for mutex: always release the lock, even if DB throws an exception.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 4 integration tests pass. README shows Redis key patterns for each service. Include redis-cli output showing keys after running the tests.
tips_for_success: Two RedisTemplate beans: String template for leaderboard/session/counters; Object template for complex Java objects
Null sentinel TTL: 5 minutes max — instrument might be added soon
try-finally for lock: redis.delete(lockKey) in finally block
Sorted Set reset: redis.delete(KEY) — DEL removes the entire key and all members
concepts_reinforced: Redis Sorted Set for real-time leaderboard (ZINCRBY, ZREVRANK, ZREVRANGE)
Manual cache-aside with RedisTemplate (get → miss → DB → set)
Null-object pattern with short TTL sentinel for missing entries
Redis Hash for partial field updates (HSET single field, HGET)
Cache stampede prevention with SETNX mutex and double-check
Multi-type Redis usage: String, Hash, Sorted Set in one service
